title: Hattie's Workshop: Puzzles and Purple Hats
summary: Hattie the Mouse, a dedicated hatmaker living on Possible Street, encounters a problem when her favorite brown cardboard puzzle develops a mysterious hole. With the help of her neighbor Mistress Mouse and a vibrant yellow-and-orange striped screwdriver, the duo manages to repair the puzzle using an unexpected solution: metal screws. Their teamwork quickly transitions from puzzle-fixing to hat-making when Mr. Swinger calls with a specific request for a purple and green cardboard hat. Using brushes, paint, glue, paper trim, and a special unicorn sticker, the two mice craft a masterpiece and deliver it to Lily's school, leaving their customer delighted with his new accessory.
audioFilename: Stars and stories - Hattie mouse makes a hat for Mr. Slinger.m4a

highlightQuote.text: They added a unicorn one... a unicorn sticker!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, let me turn on my flashlight and let me turn off this light and I'll turn off this light.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where's my flashlight?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yours? It's right behind you there. Do you see the stars? Okay, do you want to lie down for stars and stories this time? I'll lie down here, you can lie down next to me. Or do you want to sit up?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, help me turn on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll help you with that. So, in last night's story, Izzy the kiddo had gotten the help of Hattie the Mouse to make a new hat for Peacock the Wizard. And she brought the new hat to Peacock, who was so grateful and said, 'Thank you!' And now Izzy the kiddo is free to begin her next adventure. Where does she want to go next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to lie down for stars and stories. Okay, I'll lie down here. Dad?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can you tell a story?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie the Mouse lost her puzzle... and it was young and old.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait, say that again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie the Mouse and Mistress Mouse were living... and on Possible Street had a broken puzzle that had a hole. It had a hole. So Mistress Mouse had to fix it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. So what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, that's the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Hattie the Mouse had a broken puzzle with a hole in it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: With a hole and lived on Possible Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Hattie the Mouse lived on Possible Street? Yeah. And there was a hole in the puzzle? There was a hole in the puzzle. Hattie the Mouse one day was in her workshop building a puzzle. She was putting together all the pieces, and then she stopped and she said, 'Gosh, this puzzle's missing a piece! What can I do?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no. It's not missing a piece. It has a hole. And Mistress Mouse is also in the workshop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And on that day, it just so happened that Mistress Mouse was visiting.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And she was with him. No, no, no, no, no, Mistress Mouse. Hattie the Mouse brought Mistress Mouse... Mistress Mouse worked with her to fix the hole in the puzzle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Mistress Mouse says, 'Gosh, I see your problem. There's a hole in this puzzle.' And was it a little hole or a big hole?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a medium hole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a medium hole. And what was the puzzle made out of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cardboard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was made out of cardboard. And what color was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was brown.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Huh. So Mistress Mouse said, 'Gosh, we have to somehow find something that will fit this medium-sized brown cardboard hole. Maybe we could find another piece of cardboard to fit the hole. Do you think that would work?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. How about one more screw? How about one more screw? That might help with the cardboard hole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Yes, we'll need to screw it in. Do you happen to have any screws?' And Hattie Mouse said, 'No, I'm just a hatmaker. I don't use screws to make hats.' And Mistress Mouse says, 'Well, I think I have plenty of screws back at my own repair shop.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But you do have screws at this repair shop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: At Hattie Mouse's shop, she had screws too? Yeah. Okay. Hattie Mouse said, 'You know, on second thought, I think I do. Let me check in this little drawer here.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They are in there!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They are in there, look at that! And Mistress Mouse said, 'Gosh, okay, here's a screw, but we're going to need a piece of cardboard that's just the right size and shape. I wonder...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, we just need a screwdriver to screw in the screw in the hole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, do you have a screwdriver, Hattie Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do. And it's right in the... and it's right in the drawer with the screws.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Huh. Okay. I'll look in that drawer and sure enough, Mistress Mouse looked in the drawer with the screws and there was a screwdriver. So she got that out too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it was a yellow screwdriver with orange stripes. Oh yes, beautiful yellow and orange... and some orange and green lines with a straight line that was red.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mistress Mouse said, 'My goodness, this is a more beautiful screwdriver than I've ever seen. May I use it?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. It's so colorful.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then Mistress Mouse said, 'I think I have an idea about the cardboard. Do you have any scraps of paper and cardboard in your trash or your recycle bin?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You don't? Gosh, well we'll have to go out somewhere and find some. And so they went out...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, you don't need cardboard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we don't?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. You just need screws and a screwdriver.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mistress Mouse said, 'Okay, well, let me try.' So she took the puzzle and the screws and the screwdriver and she set about tinkering with it. She poked holes and she screwed in screws, and then she unscrewed them and tried screwing them in a different way. And she said, 'Hmm,' and she held up the puzzle this way and that way and looked at it from the front and the back and held it upside down and shook it and tapped on it and listened, and then she tightened one screw and loosened another and said, 'Voila! I think I fixed it.' And she handed the puzzle with its new screws all screwed in back to Hattie Mouse. And what did Hattie Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'You're welcome! I was so happy to fix this puzzle, and especially with such a beautiful screwdriver. Well, have a nice day, Hattie Mouse,' said Mistress Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mistress Mouse, let's tell another story about you and Hattie Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. What should happen in this story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mistress Mouse and Hattie Mouse make some hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they make some hats. Who are they going to make hats for this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Peacock the Wizard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. A new hat for Peacock the Wizard.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And a little bit more for the rest of... for Mrs. Swinger and Lily and Victor and Chester and Wilfred.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that's a lot of people that might need hats. Now, I think we have to finish stars and stories pretty soon. So how about we do just one hat? So which hat are we going to do? Who's it going to be for? Just for Lily?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, how about for Mr. Swinger?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mr. Swinger! And so all of a sudden, there was a ringing sound. 'Bring-bring! Bring-bring! Bring-bring!' and Mistress Mouse said, 'What's that sound?' And Hattie Mouse said, 'Oh, don't worry, that's just my telephone.' And she picked it up and she said, 'Hello? Mm-hmm. Oh, I see, mm-hmm. Oh, okay.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Mr. Swinger said, 'I need a hat for myself.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie Mouse said, 'Oh, well I'd be delighted to make a hat for you. What kind of hat would you like?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'I'd like a purple and green hat. No, no, a purple and green hat.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'A purple and green hat. And what should it be made of?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'It should be yellow and it should be made of paint and some... on a cardboard hat.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'A cardboard hat with purple and green paint. Got it.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'Okay? And you have to paint straightly with a brush. Not with your hands, okay?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Okay, anything else, Mr. Swinger?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'No, then you have to give it to me.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, okay. That sounds good. I'll get to work on it right away. Bye-bye.' And she hung up the phone and said to Mistress Mouse, 'Well, I just got a new order. Mr. Swinger wants a purple and green painted cardboard hat. What do you think about that, Mistress Mouse? Will you help me?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And just with a brush!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, and I have to paint it with a brush. Mistress Mouse said, 'Gosh, I could help you. Oh look, here's a nice piece of cardboard we could use. Where are your brushes and paints?' And Hattie Mouse said, 'Oh, they're over here. Let's get to work.' And so they set to work making a beautiful purple and green hat for Mr. Swinger. And they carefully painted the purple and green paint on with a brush.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: On some cardboard!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: On some cardboard. And they decided to add one sticker to the hat. What kind of sticker did they add?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They added a unicorn one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A unicorn sticker! And what was the finishing touch? The last little detail they added to the hat to make it just right?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, how about a glue with some paper?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They glued some paper on all around the edges of the hat to decorate it. And then they let it dry, and then Hattie the Mouse said, 'Hmm, I think it's ready. Let me just quickly try it on just to see.' And she put it on her head and looked in the mirror and said, 'Wow, I think it looks great. What do you think?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'You can bring it to Mr. Swinger, who's at Lily's school.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'That's right.' Mistress Mouse said, 'I think it looks great and it's ready, let's deliver it.' And so they brought the beautiful new hat to Lily's school where Mr. Swinger saw the hat and said, 'Wow!' He tried it on and said, 'Thank you so much, Hattie Mouse and Mistress Mouse. This is just what I wanted.' And that is the end of our story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, now do we have to turn off our flashlights?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Izzy the kiddo
characters.description: The brave young protagonist who emerged from the earthen depths alongside the moles to aid Hattie, Isabella remains a steadfast visitor whose melodic songs of courage bolster her friends.

characters.name: Peacock the Wizard
characters.description: Once a deep-ocean companion to Seeker, this vibrant bird wizard now serves as the charismatic host of the magical castle’s grand show. From a cozy nest atop a soaring tower, they orchestrate wonder through balloon school arts—transmuting rubber into succulent strawberries—while proudly sporting a magnificent hat gifted by Hattie and Izzy. A playful visionary of dreamer magic and a tireless seeker of rare diamond-shaped potions, they use their stage to beckon heroes into a world of soaring imagination, often dispatching them on whimsical quests to recover enchanted new hats for their growing collection.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Mr. Swinger
characters.description: A customer who calls Hattie to order a custom-painted purple and green cardboard hat.

characters.name: Mrs. Swinger
characters.description: A character mentioned as someone who might need a new hat.

characters.name: Lily
characters.description: The world’s tallest mouse is a guardian scholar of monumental power and studious grace, serving as the living pillar of a communal house where he hoists the massive roof with peerless strength. Between shifts at the Pepperoni Street tape store and classes at the local school, this friend of Mistress Mouse finds quiet joy building with Legos or coloring in the baby lounge alongside his many brothers. Adorned in his signature shell necklace and a fresh bespoke hat from Haddy, he remains a gentle soul whose immense physical duty is matched only by his dedicated, playful heart.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Chester
characters.description: A mouse of many suggestions who auditions a repertoire of sounds for his worm friend, now counted among the many figures awaiting a hat of his own.

characters.name: Wilfred
characters.description: One of the many characters mentioned as needing a hat.

places.name: Possible Street
places.description: The street where Hattie the Mouse and Mistress Mouse live.

places.name: Hattie's workshop
places.description: The sunlit cradle of Hattie’s grand adventure, this workshop remains a sanctuary of craftsmanship and logic where she shapes her signature hats and untangles complex puzzles—now serving as a warm, vital refuge for the islanders after their home was lost to the frost.

places.name: Mistress Mouse's repair shop
places.description: The location where Mistress Mouse keeps her tools, including various screws.

places.name: Lily's school
places.description: The destination where Hattie and Mistress Mouse deliver a custom hat to Mr. Swinger.
